content.full_text:
GADDI H. VASQUEZ
CHAIRMAN OF THE BOARD OF SUPERVISORS
SUPERVISOR, THIRO OISTRICT
ROBERT E. THOMAS HALL OF ADMINISTRATION
lo ctvtc cENTER PLAZA, sANTA ANA, CALIFORNTA 927Ot-4O6r
PHONE: (714) a34-333O
November 4, 1991
Mr. Ben Davidian
Chairman
Fair Political Practice Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814
Dear Ben:
As a result of redistricting, I would like to notifu the constituents which
I no longer represent, and those which I now represent, of the recent
changes in Supervisorial Districts. Unfortunately, this would involve a
mass mailing or more than 200letters, and I'm not clear as to whether this
is a permitted use of public funds.
Because this letter would be inforrrational in nature, and not partisan or
campaign literature, I believe that I, as a public official, have the
implied power to do such a mailing, as long as it is factually correct, and
neutral in nature. Informing voters of the identity of their elected
representative does fulfill a public purpose.
As the head of the FPPC, I would appreciate your opinion regarding the
legality of such a mailing. Specifically, should I, as a County
Supervisor, be allowed to send a mailer at public expense to former, and./or
new constituents of the Third Supenrisorial District, notifring them of the
recent changes in Supervisorial Boundaries?
Ben, I appreciate you taking a look at this, and I'm looking forward to
your opinion.
Sincerely,
e/
*azH'
GADDI H. VASQUEZ
Chairman of the Board of Supervisors
Supervisor, Third District
GHV:dwa
&

sections.question: Specifically, should I, as a County Supervisor, be allowed to send a mailer at public expense to former, and/or new constituents of the Third Supervisorial District, notifying them of the recent changes in Supervisorial Boundaries?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a County Supervisor may use public funds to mail informational letters to constituents notifying them of redistricting changes to their supervisorial district boundaries.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with those headers are present, but the question is clearly stated in the final paragraph. No FPPC advice or conclusion provided in this document.

embedding.summary: Gaddi H. Vasquez, a County Supervisor, requests FPPC guidance on whether he may use public funds to send mass mailings to constituents informing them of redistricting changes affecting the Third Supervisorial District.
